compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
REBAR-DEBUG DIAGNOSIS (2026-07-21)

Reproduction/localization: current origin/main src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T8.md says the overlay applies to plans containing "output schemas, enums" without requiring an LLM consumer/producer. The same rubric then asks the reviewer to probe any referenced schema/enum. There is no deterministic applicability guard before the agentic criterion runs.

Root cause: applicability is encoded only as ambiguous prompt prose. Generic typed data vocabulary is therefore a positive LLM-surface signal, so ordinary dataclasses, sentinels, and stdlib enums can engage T8. The existing ANTI-FP sentence requires concrete evidence after engagement but does not prevent false engagement.

Minimal corrective seam: narrow the T8 applicability sentence to schemas/enums consumed by or emitted from an LLM/tool/reviewer contract, add explicit deterministic-code counterexamples, and pin both abstention and a true LLM-schema positive case in criterion eval/unit coverage. Preserve any ordinary data-shape finding under a non-overlay criterion.

No code changed during this diagnostic run.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: Landed in Gerrit change 864. Narrowed T8 applicability to LLM-consumed or LLM-emitted schemas and added deterministic negative/positive regression coverage. Verified and LLM-reviewed before merge.

compiled_state.description:
## Situation encountered
While plan-reviewing the code-health metrics epic (839b-f6d2), the T8 overlay criterion — "LLM / prompt structural-completeness probe" — fired BLOCKING findings on two tickets that define NO LLM/agent system:
- 3ba0 (jscpd duplication analyzer — a deterministic external-CLI shell-out)
- 0734 (git_metrics migration — deterministic metric readers)

Both are plain Python metric code. They have no prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, or LLM output schemas.

## Why it mis-fired
T8`s applicability gate (`src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T8.md`) reads:
  "OVERLAY — apply when the plan defines an LLM/agent system (prompts, sub-agents, reviewers, output schemas, enums)."
The plans merely mention ordinary data shapes — the `AnalyzerResult` dataclass (fields loc/complexity/duplication), the `Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` sentinel, and an `accruing_since` value. The overlay treated a plain dataclass / value object as an "LLM output schema/enum" and engaged.

## Evidence (actual T8 blocking findings)
- 0734: "`AnalyzerResult` (defined in 3b30 with fields loc/complexity/duplication) has no field for git-commit history" and "`module_size_trend` ... the plan specifies no computation protocol."
- 3ba0: "the plan mandates `Unavailable(reason, accruing_since)` but never specifies where the `accruing_since` value comes from ... there is no `MetricSpec` context available to provide it."
These are ordinary data-shape/spec observations (some are legitimate design concerns), but they are NOT LLM-prompt structural gaps — they were routed through the wrong criterion, mislabeled, and counted toward a BLOCK.

## Impact
An LLM-overlay criterion blocking deterministic, non-LLM plans inflates the blocking count, mislabels the concern, and (with other snapshot-grounding FPs) prevented these tickets from earning a plan-review attestation. It erodes trust in the gate for the broad polyglot client base rebar targets, most of whose tickets are not LLM systems.

## Expected behavior
- The T8 applicability gate must NOT engage on a plan whose surface is purely deterministic (no prompts / sub-agents / reviewers / LLM output-schemas-consumed-by-a-model). A typed dataclass (`AnalyzerResult`) or a value sentinel (`Unavailable`) is NOT an "LLM output schema/enum".
- Tighten the gate so "output schemas, enums" is scoped to schemas/enums CONSUMED BY OR EMITTED FOR AN LLM (structured-output contracts, tool schemas, reviewer verdict vocabularies), not any Python dataclass/enum.
- Genuine data-shape-completeness gaps should surface under an appropriate NON-overlay criterion (e.g. the E-series structural/coherence checks) so they are correctly labeled and weighted — not laundered through T8.

## Acceptance criteria
- [ ] A fixture plan defining only plain dataclasses/enums + a deterministic CLI adapter (no LLM surface) yields ZERO T8 findings. PROVE: an eval/unit over the fixture asserts T8 abstains (not-applicable).
- [ ] The T8 applicability-gate wording in `plan_review_T8.md` is narrowed to LLM-consumed/emitted schemas/enums, with an explicit ANTI-FP note ("a plain dataclass / value object / stdlib Enum used by deterministic code is NOT in scope").
- [ ] A regression fixture derived from 3ba0 / 0734 (metric adapter + migration) does not draw a T8 block.
- [ ] Any genuine spec gap the old T8 findings named (e.g. accruing_since source) still surfaces — but under a non-overlay criterion, correctly labeled.

## Files likely touched
- src/rebar/llm/reviewers/plan_review_T8.md (applicability gate + anti-FP)
- src/rebar/llm/plan_review/criteria_routing.json (if applies_at/posture needs adjustment)
- a plan-review eval/regression fixture + test (docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/ or tests/)

## Provenance
Discovered while decomposing + plan-reviewing epic 839b-f6d2 (code-health metrics portability). Part of a broader set of plan-review FP classes found in that session (the others: snapshot-grounding of depends_on-created symbols, and verdict variance under a moving origin/main snapshot).
compiled_state.title: Plan-review T8 overlay over-fires on non-LLM tickets (dataclass/enum vocabulary misread as an LLM output-schema)
